posts._note: Show 1 key model or system inside the guide. Build authority. Teach something real.
posts.linkedin:
Most digital product launches fail at the same stage: before the product exists.

People build first, then try to find buyers. That's backwards.

The L.A.U.N.C.H.™ Framework — the system inside the 30-Day Digital Product Launch Plan — fixes this:

→ L — List Audience Needs: Before building anything, you identify the exact pain your product solves and validate that people will pay for the solution. Market research built into Day 1.

→ A — Align MVP: Define the minimum viable product — the smallest version of your idea that delivers the core result. Scope creep is the #1 reason products never ship.

→ U — Unlock Market Proof: Get 3–5 people to say "I'd buy this" before building it. Pre-validation eliminates the risk of building something nobody wants.

→ N — Nurture Pre-Launch: Build a warm audience before launch day. Email list, social posts, waitlist — all templated and mapped in the guide.

→ C — Create & Launch: Build the product using the included production templates. Launch on Day 30 with a complete sales process already set up.

→ H — Harvest & Iterate: Collect feedback, optimize conversion, and set up the next product cycle.

Every phase has templates, scripts, and a day-by-day task list.

$37. 18 pages.

👉 https://publixion.com/guides/30-day-digital-product-launch

#DigitalProducts #ProductLaunch #Solopreneur #Publixion

posts._note: Show how a specific person would use this guide step by step. LinkedIn + Facebook only.
posts.linkedin:
Here's exactly how a freelance copywriter used this guide to launch her first digital product.

She'd been thinking about creating a "client onboarding template pack" for 6 months. Every time she sat down to build it, she didn't know where to start.

Day 1–3 (L — List Audience Needs): She used the audience research template in the guide to ask 10 copywriters in her network one question: "What's the most painful part of onboarding new clients?" Consistent answer: scope creep and unclear briefs.

Day 4–7 (A — Align MVP): She scoped the smallest useful version — 5 templates, not 20. The guide's MVP framework kept her from over-building.

Day 8–12 (U — Unlock Market Proof): She posted a waitlist. 23 signups in 4 days. Market validated before she built a single template.

Day 13–22 (N + C — Nurture & Create): She built the 5 templates. Posted 3 pre-launch content pieces using the guide's templates. Set up her Gumroad page.

Day 30 — Launch: 23 waitlist members got the email. 9 purchased at $47. $423 in the first 6 hours.

Within 60 days: 87 total sales. $4,089 in revenue from one template pack built in 30 days.

👉 Get the guide: https://publixion.com/guides/30-day-digital-product-launch

#DigitalProducts #CaseStudy #ProductLaunch #Publixion

posts._note: Handle the top doubts: price, time, skill level, whether it will work for them.
posts.linkedin:
Before you move on — let me handle the objections.

"$37 is too much for a guide."
A single digital product sale at $27 pays for this guide. You need exactly one customer to recoup the investment. Most people who follow the system make that back on launch day.

"I don't have an audience to launch to."
The guide covers this. Phase N (Nurture) is specifically about building a small, warm audience from scratch in the 2 weeks before launch. You don't need thousands of followers. You need 20–30 engaged people.

"I don't know if my idea is good enough."
Phase U (Unlock Market Proof) solves this before you build anything. You validate demand with real people before investing time in creation.

"I don't have time to build a product in 30 days."
The guide is designed for people with full-time jobs. The daily tasks average 45–60 minutes. It's structured to fit real schedules.

"I've bought business guides before that didn't work."
This is an execution document, not an inspiration document. Every chapter ends with a specific task list. The result is measured by whether you ship — not by how motivated you feel.

👉 https://publixion.com/guides/30-day-digital-product-launch

#DigitalProducts #Objections #Solopreneur #Publixion
